Legislative Chamber, Room 250
City Hall, 1 Dr. Carlton B. Goodlett Place
San Francisco, CA 94102-4689
SHAMANN WALTON, PRESIDENT

Inaugural Meeting
Monday, January 9, 2023 - 10:00 AM

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
Angela Calvillo, Clerk of the Board
Volume 118     Number 1
Committee Membership Meeting Days
First-named Supervisor is Chair, Second-named Supervisor is Vice-Chair of the Committee.
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
Members Present: Connie Chan, Matt Dorsey, Joel Engardio, Rafael Mandelman, Myrna Melgar, Aaron 
Peskin, Dean Preston, Hillary Ronen, Ahsha Safai, Catherine Stefani, and Shamann 
Walton
The Board of Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco met for the inaugural session, 
in-person with remote access and public comment also available via telephone, on Monday, January 
9, 2023, with Angela Calvillo, Clerk of the Board, calling the meeting to order. 
Clerk Calvillo called the meeting to order at 10:03 a.m.
Remote Access to Information and Participation
The Board of Supervisors and its committees convene hybrid meetings that allow in-person 
attendance, remote access, and public comment via teleconference. Visit the SFGovTV website 
(www.sfgovtv.org) to stream the live meetings and watch meetings on demand or watch live meetings 
on San Francisco Cable Channels 26, 28, 78 or 99 (depending on your provider). Members of the 
public may provide public comment in-person or remotely via teleconference (detailed instructions 
available at: https://sfbos.org/remote-meeting-call). 
Members of the public may submit their comments by email to: bos@sfgov.org; all comments 
received will be made a part of the official record. Board of Supervisors Regular Meetings begin at 
2:00 p.m. on Tuesdays. Committee agendas and their associated documents are available at 
https://sfbos.org/meetings/42.
Please visit the Board’s website (www.sfbos.org) regularly to be updated on the current situation as it 
affects the legislative process. For more information contact the Office of the Clerk of the Board at 
(415) 554-5184 or bos@sfgov.org.
ROLL CALL AND PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE
On the call of the roll, Supervisors Chan, Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Melgar, Peskin, Preston, 
Ronen, Safai, Stefani, and Walton were noted present.  
All Members of the Board were present.
Page 1 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
COMMUNICATIONS
Clerk Calvillo reported that she was in receipt of a communication from the Department of Elections, 
certifying the candidates for the Office of the Members of the Board of Supervisors who received a 
majority of votes cast at the election held on November 8, 2022, and have been declared elected to 
that office, namely: 
District 2 - Catherine Stefani
District 4 - Joel Engardio
District 6 - Matt Dorsey
District 8 - Rafael Mandelman
District 10 - Shamann Walton
Clerk Calvillo informed the Board Members that she had received a communication from the 
Controller confirming that certification of surety bond requirements were established for all newly 
elected and re-elected Board Members, pursuant to Administrative Code, Article IX, Section 
16.122(a)(e).
Clerk Calvillo then stated that, pursuant to Charter Section 2.116, on the eighth day of January in 
odd numbered years, the Board of Supervisors shall elect one of its Members as President of the 
Board. If January 8th falls on a holiday or weekend, as it did this year, Rule 6.1 of the Board’s Rules 
of Order requires the Board to schedule its organization and Presidential election at 10:00 a.m. the 
following business day. Today’s meeting satisfies these requirements. 
Finally, Clerk Calvillo reported that, as of 12:00 noon on January 8, 2023, there were no vacancies in 
the District offices as each newly elected and re-elected Members of the Board were officially sworn 
in and assumed their duties as public servants, continuing the tradition that binds officials across 
more than 100 years of San Francisco history in service to the residents of San Francisco.
OATH OF OFFICE - MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
Clerk Calvillo introduced the Honorable Anne-Christine Massullo, Presiding Judge, Superior Court of 
California, County of San Francisco. Judge Massullo requested the newly elected and re-elected 
Members of the Board to stand; whereupon Judge Massullo administered the Oath of Office as 
Members of the Board of Supervisors and Commisioners of the County Transportation Authority.
Page 2 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
NOMINATIONS FOR THE OFFICE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD 
OF SUPERVISORS
Clerk Calvillo provided the nominating principles, as follows:
- Members may nominate one person, either themselves or another Member;
- Nominees state on the record if they accept the nomination;
- Nominees do not need to vacate the Chamber;
- Nominees may withdraw their own nomination without a second at any time;
- At completion of nominations, the nominations will be closed;
- Members may vote for themselves;
- The Board may adopt a motion to reopen nominations with a majority vote; and
- If nominations are reopened, those Members who have not nominated may do so or a Member may 
withdraw their previous nomination and nominate a different Member. 
Supervisor Ronen nominated Supervisor Walton.
Supervisor Stefani nominated Supervisor Mandelman.
Supervisor Melgar nominated Supervisor Chan.
Clerk Calvillo inquired if there were any further nominations. There being no response, the 
nominations for the Office of the President of the Board of Supervisors were then closed.
GENERAL PUBLIC COMMENT 
Del Seymour; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Paulina Fayer; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
William Jake; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Yolanda Williams; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Joanna Hernandez; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Joe Kunzler; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Marie Hurabiell; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Naj Daniels; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Annabelle Ibanez; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Cheryl Traverse; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Jared Goldpine; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Theordore Randolph; expressed concerns regarding affordable housing.
Dr. Monique LeSarre; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Venetia Margarita; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Brenda Barros; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Fred Winograd; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Gloria Berry; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Alice Savior; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Marina; spoke in support of Supervisor Rafael Mandelman for President of the Board.
Pastor Sweetie Kigata; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Gaynor Siataga; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Cheryl Thornton; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Alexandra Grainer; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Jameel Patterson; spoke in support of Supervisor Shamann Walton for President of the Board.
Page 3 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
ELECTION OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF SUPERVISORS
Supervisor Walton accepted the nomination.
Supervisor Mandelman accepted the nomination.
Supervisor Chan accepted the nomination.
Clerk Calvillo provided the principles for the election of the President of the Board of Supervisors, as 
follows:
- Voting occurs in alphabetical order;
- There are no abstentions or passes;
- When called upon, Members vote by stating the name of their preference from among the 
nominees; 
- The first nominee to receive a majority vote will be the President of the Board;
- The nominee with the least number of votes is not obligated to withdraw their name nor will they be 
automatically withdrawn from the ballot;
- If no nominee received a majority of the votes, they will proceed to the next round of voting; and
- If nominations are reopened, those Members who have not nominated may do so or a Member may 
withdraw their previous nomination and nominate a different Member. 
The roll was called on the nominations of Supervisors Chan, Mandelman, and Walton.
ROUND 1 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 2 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 3 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round.
Page 4 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
ROUND 4 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 5 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 6 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 7 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 8 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 9 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in nomination 
were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 10 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
Page 5 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
ROUND 11 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 12 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
Supervisor Chan, seconded by Supervisor Peskin, moved to reopen nominations for the election of 
the President of the Board of Supervisors. The motion carried by the following vote:
   Ayes: 9 - Chan, Mandelman, Melgar, Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Stefani, Walton
   Noes: 2 - Dorsey, Engardio
Supervisor Peskin nominated himself. 
Supervisor Mandelman moved, seconded by Supervisor Peskin, moved to recess the meeting 
beginning at 11:33 a.m. The motion carried without objection.
Clerk Calvillo reconvened the meeting at 11:48 a.m.
ROUND 13 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Chan: 2 - Chan, Melgar
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 5 - Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
   Peskin: 0 - None
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 14 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Peskin: 3 - Chan, Melgar, Peskin
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 4 - Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
   Chan: 0 - None
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
ROUND 15 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Peskin: 3 - Chan, Melgar, Peskin
   Mandelman: 4 - Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Stefani
   Walton: 4 - Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
   Chan: 0 - None
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
Page 6 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
ROUND 16 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Peskin: 4 - Chan, Mandelman, Melgar, Peskin
   Mandelman: 3 - Dorsey, Engardio, Stefani
   Walton: 4 - Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
   Chan: 0 - None
No nominee received a majority of the votes; the Board proceeded to the next round. 
Supervisor Ronen, seconded by Supervisor Chan, moved to recess the meeting beginning at 11:57 
a.m. The motion carried by the following vote:
   Ayes: 11 - Chan, Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Melgar, Peskin, Preston, Ronen, Safai, Stefani, 
Walton
Clerk Calvillo reconvened the meeting at 12:09 p.m.
ROUND 17 - Vote to select the President of the Board of Supervisors among the names in 
nomination were declared, as follows:
   Peskin: 7 - Chan, Dorsey, Engardio, Mandelman, Melgar, Peskin, Stefani
   Walton: 4 - Preston, Ronen, Safai, Walton
   Chan: 0 - None
   Mandelman: 0 - None
President Peskin received a majority of the votes in Round 17 and requested the honor of having 
Supervisors Chan, Mandelman and Walton escort him to the dais as he assumed the chair. 
PRESIDENT AARON PESKIN ASSUMED THE CHAIR
President Peskin presented the ancestral homeland acknowledgement of the Ramaytush Ohlone, 
who are the original inhabitants of the San Francisco Peninsula, pursuant to Board Rule 4.7.1.
REMARKS FROM THE MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN 
FRANCISCO, THE HONORABLE LONDON N. BREED
Privilege of the floor was granted unanimously to Tom Paulino (Mayor’s Office) who reported on the 
Mayor’s attendance.  
Mayor London N. Breed congratulated President Peskin and all of the newly elected and re-elected 
Members of the Board of Supervisors. She shared the various issues and challenges ahead for the 
City - including homelessness, open air drug use and housing - and shared her desire to continue 
working with the Members of the Board to ensure the success of the City’s future.
REMARKS BY THE HONORABLE MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF 
SUPERVISORS
President Peskin called on each of the newly elected, re-elected, and continuing Members of the 
Board of Supervisors and afforded each an opportunity for brief remarks and introduction of family 
members and guests in attendance.
Page 7 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
1/9/2023Board of Supervisors Meeting Minutes
REMARKS BY THE HONORABLE PRESIDENT OF THE BOARD OF 
SUPERVISORS
President Peskin recognized the many elected officials and department heads in attendance for 
today’s meeting. President Peskin thanked Supervisor Walton for his work and efforts during his 
tenure as President of the Board during the last term and bestowed a plaque recognizing his service. 
President Peskin then humbly expressed his appreciation for the Members of the Board for electing 
him President and encouraged the continued collaboration between the District offices. He looked 
forward to working together as they anticipate navigating the many challenges facing San Francisco.
ADJOURNMENT
There being no further business, the Board adjourned at the hour of 1:27 p.m.
N.B.  The Minutes of this meeting set forth all actions taken by the Board of Supervisors on the 
matters stated, but not necessarily the chronological sequence in which the matters were taken up.
Approved by the Board of Supervisors on February 14, 2023.
Angela Calvillo, Clerk of the Board
Page 8 Printed at  5:40 pm on 3/14/24City and County of San Francisco
